mission.title: tikv: metrics: generate Grafana dashboards with python
mission.description:
### What is changed and how it works?

Issue Number: Close #12796

What's Changed:

Lists of tikv-detail row panels:

- [x] Duration
- [x] Cluster
- [x] Errors
- [x] Server
- [x] gRPC
- [x] Thread CPU
- [x] TTL
- [x] PD
- [x] IO Breakdown
- [x] Raft Waterfall
- [x] Raft IO
- [x] Raft Propose
- [x] Raft Process
- [x] Raft Message
- [x] Raft Admin
- [x] Raft Log
- [x] Local reader
- [x] Unified Read Pool
- [x] Storage
- [x] Flow Control
- [x] Scheduler - $command
- [x] Scheduler
- [x] GC
- [x] Snapshot
- [x] Task
- [x] Coprocessor Overview
- [x] Coprocessor Detail
- [x] Threads
- [x] RocksDB - $db
- [x] Raft Engine
- [x] Titan - $titan_db
- [x] Pessimistic Locking
- [x] Point In Time Restore
- [x] Resolved-TS
- [x] Memory
- [x] Backup & Import
- [x] Encryption
- [x] Backup Log
- [x] Slow Trend Statistics

resolution.summary:
> Really appreciate the work, but would the API flexible and easy enough for adding a new panel including plugins and new types of panels as discussed in #12796?

Yes, it's more flexible than grafonnet. In fact, the dashboard generated by this PR looks identical to the original tikv_detail dashboard.
steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request

Awesome, have we supported this for other dashboard like TiDB, PD, CDC, etc too? @overvenus 
> Awesome, have we supported this for other dashboard like TiDB, PD, CDC, etc too? @overvenus

Not yet, I think it's fairly simple, because most the translation (from JSON to Python) is done by ChatGPT. I will share my prompt to folks working on these projects.
For TiDB, we have already used Jsonnet for other metrics, so let's adapt it. 

The prompt I used:

I burned all my Claude code tokens, then I used my Codex to take over the reset jobs. It records all progress, making it easy to switch models and tools to complete the work.

My PR: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/65764
